picks.reasoning: Barcelona at 1.5 — a 66.7% implied probability — is significantly overcooked for a UCL knockout tie against Atlético Madrid, arguably Europe's finest defensive unit. In Champions League eliminator football, Atlético are historically expert at neutralising La Liga superiority. A recent CTA controversy over a Gerard Martín red card that was never given confirms this fixture is bitterly contested and far from a Barcelona walkover. Add the first-leg dynamics — both sides cautious, neither wanting to concede away goals — and the draw at 5.0 (20% implied) is materially undervalued. True draw probability: 25%.

PSG are in excellent domestic form — Dembélé has been one of the players of the season in Europe — and home advantage at the Parc des Princes is real. But 25% implied for Liverpool feels marginally low for a club with their Champions League pedigree. Arne Slot's side have been resilient in European away fixtures this campaign and have the squad depth to punish PSG on the counter. In a two-legged tie where Liverpool will prioritise a clean sheet, their away win probability is closer to 29%. The edge is modest but the reasoning is sound for a pick at this price.
